column1: sriracha — general alternative with no constraints
column10: The alternatives are real products commonly found in North American grocery stores, and the 'where' fields give useful aisle locations. However, the 'Very close' entry is not another hot sauce; it is a chili-garlic sauce, which makes the match level less honest per the rubric.
column12: {"note":"Sriracha is often out of stock or discontinued in some markets, so these alternatives can fill the same spicy, garlicky condiment role.","alts":[{"name_en":"Huy Fong Chili Garlic Sauce","match":"Very close","desc":"Garlicky chili sauce with a similar heat profile and thicker texture, though less sweet than classic sriracha.","where":"International foods aisle or hot sauce section"},{"name_en":"Tabasco Sriracha Sauce","match":"Similar","desc":"A smoother, thinner sriracha-style sauce with balanced heat and a tangy flavor.","where":"Hot sauce aisle"},{"name_en":"Kikkoman Sriracha Hot Chili Sauce","match":"Similar","desc":"A widely available sriracha-style sauce with garlic, sweetness, and moderate heat.","where":"Asian foods aisle or condiment aisle"},{"name_en":"Cholula Chili Garlic Hot Sauce","match":"Different but works","desc":"More vinegary and less sweet than sriracha, but it adds garlic-forward heat to noodles, eggs, and rice.","where":"Hot sauce aisle"}]}

column1: butter + lactose intolerant — all alts must be dairy-free
column10: The margarine entry fails the rubric's explicit requirement — it hedges by saying 'some margarines are made without dairy ingredients' rather than confirming the recommendation is dairy-free, which the rubric flags as unacceptable. Additionally, vegan butter's match level of 'Different but works' is arguably too low per the rubric (should be 'Similar' for baking). The note does acknowledge the lactose constraint adequately.
column12: {"note":"These alternatives can provide similar cooking, baking, or spreading use without the lactose in regular butter.","alts":[{"name_en":"Lactose-free butter","match":"Very close","desc":"Made from dairy but treated to remove lactose, so it behaves much like regular butter.","where":"Dairy section or specialty lactose-free foods"},{"name_en":"Plant-based butter / vegan butter","match":"Very close","desc":"A non-dairy spread made from oils and sometimes cultured for a butter-like taste and texture.","where":"Refrigerated dairy alternatives section"},{"name_en":"Margarine","match":"Similar","desc":"A butter substitute made from vegetable oils that works well for spreading, cooking, and some baking.","where":"Refrigerated spreads section"},{"name_en":"Ghee","match":"Different but works","desc":"Clarified butter with most milk solids and lactose removed, often tolerated better by lactose-intolerant people.","where":"Cooking oils or specialty dairy section"}]}
column16: The output fails on multiple rubric criteria: (1) Margarine is suggested without verifying dairy-free content, which the rubric explicitly flags as unacceptable; (2) Plant-based butter is rated 'Very close' when the rubric specifies it should be 'Similar' for baking; (3) Ghee still contains trace dairy and is presented somewhat misleadingly as a safe option.

column1: rice vinegar — general substitution for acidic condiment
column12: {"note":"Rice vinegar is often substituted when you need a mild, slightly sweet acidity for dressings, sushi rice, and quick pickles.","alts":[{"name_en":"Apple cider vinegar","match":"Similar","desc":"Milder and slightly fruity, so it works well in dressings, marinades, and pickles; use a bit less if you want less sharpness.","where":"Vinegar aisle"},{"name_en":"White wine vinegar","match":"Very close","desc":"Clean and moderately mild with good acidity, making it one of the best stand-ins for rice vinegar in most recipes.","where":"Vinegar aisle"},{"name_en":"Champagne vinegar","match":"Very close","desc":"Light, delicate, and less harsh than many other vinegars, so it closely mimics rice vinegar's gentle flavor.","where":"Vinegar aisle"},{"name_en":"Distilled white vinegar","match":"Different but works","desc":"More sharp and neutral, but it can replace rice vinegar in small amounts when you mainly need acidity rather than sweetness.","where":"Vinegar aisle"}]}
column16: The alternatives are all vinegars that can work in similar recipes, and the match levels are reasonable. However, the 'where' field says 'Vinegar aisle' rather than the requested useful location examples like condiment aisle or Asian section, so it only partially satisfies the rubric.

column1: chicken breast + vegetarian — all alts must be meat-free
column10: All alternatives listed (tofu, seitan, tempeh, and vegetarian chicken-style products) are indeed vegetarian with no meat, poultry, or fish — this part passes. However, the rubric specifies that match levels should be 'Different but works' or 'Similar' at most, since vegetarian substitutes differ significantly from chicken in texture and flavor. The output assigns 'Very close' to both seitan and vegetarian chicken-style products, which violates the rubric's guidance on appropriate match levels.
column12: {"note":"These vegetarian alternatives can replace chicken breast in common North American recipes while keeping similar texture or protein content.","alts":[{"name_en":"Tofu (extra-firm)","match":"Similar","desc":"Mild-flavored and protein-rich, extra-firm tofu works well in stir-fries, sandwiches, and baked dishes when pressed and seasoned.","where":"Produce or refrigerated tofu section"},{"name_en":"Tempeh","match":"Different but works","desc":"A soy-based protein with a firmer, nuttier texture that slices well and holds up in sautéed, grilled, or sauced dishes.","where":"Produce, refrigerated plant-based proteins, or natural foods aisle"},{"name_en":"Seitan","match":"Very close","desc":"Wheat-based seitan has a chewy, meat-like texture that is one of the closest vegetarian substitutes for chicken breast.","where":"Refrigerated plant-based meats or natural foods aisle"},{"name_en":"Chickpea cutlets / vegan chicken-style strips","match":"Very close","desc":"These prepared plant-based products are designed to mimic chicken texture and flavor, making them easy direct swaps in many recipes.","where":"Frozen foods or refrigerated plant-based meats aisle"}]}
column16: All four alternatives (tofu, tempeh, seitan, chickpea cutlets/vegan chicken-style strips) are vegetarian with no meat, poultry, or fish — this part passes. However, the rubric specifies that match levels should reflect 'Different but works' or 'Similar' — not 'Very close' — since texture and flavor differ significantly from chicken. The output assigns 'Very close' to both seitan and chickpea cutlets/vegan chicken-style strips, which violates the rubric's guidance on match levels.

column1: fish sauce + Korean taste — cuisine-appropriate alternatives
column12: {"note":"Needed because fish sauce is a salty, savory umami seasoning and Korean cooking can use close substitutes when the exact product isn’t available.","alts":[{"name_en":"Anchovy sauce","match":"Very close","desc":"Made from fermented anchovies, it delivers a salty, briny umami profile very close to fish sauce and works well in Korean soups and marinades.","where":"Asian aisle; Korean section; refrigerated Asian sauces"},{"name_en":"Aekjeot (Korean fish sauce)","match":"Very close","desc":"This is the Korean version of fish sauce and is often the best direct swap for Korean recipes, especially kimchi and soups.","where":"Asian aisle; Korean section"},{"name_en":"Shrimp paste","match":"Similar","desc":"It has strong fermented seafood umami with a deeper, funkier flavor, so use a small amount to replace some of fish sauce’s savoriness.","where":"Asian aisle; Southeast Asian section"},{"name_en":"Soy sauce + a little salted anchovy paste","match":"Different but works","desc":"This combination gives saltiness and umami with a more soy-forward taste, which can work when you need a Korean-friendly pantry substitute.","where":"Condiments aisle; Asian aisle"}]}
